# Verification Report

**Verdict:** FAIL
**Claims reviewed:** 6
**Sources reviewed:** 0
**Open agenda items:** 3

## Findings
1. [HIGH] The Trump administration likely continues a deregulatory approach to AI-driven automation, emphasizing competitive market advancement. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
2. [HIGH] Sectors like white-collar jobs, manufacturing, and customer service are expected to experience disruption due to AI adoption. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
3. [HIGH] Investment implications suggest underweighting labor-intensive staffing sectors and overweighting AI infrastructure companies. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
4. [HIGH] The Trump administration in 2026 focuses on workforce reduction strategies leveraging AI. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
5. [HIGH] AI-driven automation is notably disrupting white-collar jobs in professional services and education. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
6. [HIGH] Investors are shifting capital towards companies providing AI infrastructure. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
